He says that it’s the best place for mulling over new ideas! You know, my boy is a brilliant physicist; he likes to look at the water, it soothes him! Hello, Gloria. Can we talk? Yes, of course. What happened? Well, the thing is... I wanted to meet your son, so I made some inquiries... What for?! I would have introduced you myself! I mean, he’s very busy, so I’m not sure he would have found time, but there was no need to go asking anyone... Gloria, I know your son died 5 years ago. What? Don’t be silly... My son is alive! No, Gloria. Dr. Binet told me everything... About Malcolm and your illness... You should start taking your medication again. Gloria? Malcolm is... dead?.. But... what about me? I’m so sorry... What am I going to do, Evan?.. I... I can’t live without him... You should get back into treatment, Gloria! Why? Why should I get treatment if Malcolm is gone? Tell me, Evan, why do I need all this? Gloria, I... WHY? I... I don’t want it! Listen! I! Don’t! Want it! Gloria, wait! Where are you going... That was a very, very, very bad joke, Evan! I didn’t think you were capable of something like this. Never do such a thing to me or anyone else again! Hi, mom! How are you? Malcolm, is that you? What a surprise! What are you doing here? How did you get in? I asked a policeman where I could find you and told him who I was, and he let me through. Great, isn’t it? Now I can finally see what you do here! Malcolm, I’m so happy! Come on, I’ll show you everything! Look what a beautiful Shredder we have here, Malcolm! It works around the clock, disposing of the spoiled and processed applications. Wow! I’ve never seen anything like it! Didn’t they teach you about it in Bureaucracy class? They did, but it’s much more interesting to see it in person! But they collected money for a tour around the Ministry, they were supposed to show you everything! Well, mom, you know how it works... They took the tour money and sent us on a field trip to dig turnips. What?! I had no idea! I will file a complaint! Remind me, what was the number of your school? School No. 6056 How could you forget? Remember how we laughed when we noticed that it’s made up of grandpa’s age and your year of birth? That can’t be right... Your grandfather died at 25, and I'm not that old! School No. 2562 2562. You used to know those numbers by heart! You said that was exactly how much you had to pay the principal to get me a place. Do you have a fever, darling? What are you talking about? I never bribed anyone! School No. 0261 0261, I think. What do you mean, you had no idea? Don’t you remember how those turnips saved us from starving? Sorry, son... Of course I remember! It’s just my memory isn’t as good as it used to be. It’s alright, mom. I’m just glad everything is okay now. You know what, let’s go to the movies after work, like we used to! They’re all about war now, and I don’t like those... We can try and find a civilian show! Do you remember what your favorite movie was as a kid? We must have watched it a hundred times, at least! Attack of the Killer Turnips Turnips? You couldn’t even look at them after that winter when we had nothing else to eat! Do you need to go get some fresh air? The Human Caterpillar Malcolm, I'm talking about when you were a child! The Human Caterpillar is a film for adults... You couldn’t have seen it back then! Maybe you really should go get some air. Pride and Prejudice and the Leader Right! You were in love with the main character! You used to hide a picture of her under your pillow and stare at it at night. Mom, don’t... It’s alright, Malcolm, all boys are the same at that age. You’ll see when you have kids of your own. I hope I’ll still be around to tell them funny stories about their dad! The Sneaky Snitch That’s strange... What’s strange, mom? It’s strange that you forgot what your own favorite movie is, and now you're lying to my face about it. I’m not lying! It was definitely “Snitch”! The Sneaky Snitch came out three years ago. How could you have watched it as a child? I think we need to have a serious talk about your behavior! Speaking of movies... Remember when you left your favorite book in the movie theater? We spent such a long time searching for it that we were arrested for violating the curfew! I can’t remember the title... Advice for a Young Partisan Malcolm, are you feeling alright? Yes, why? I think you’re delusional! We’ve never had books like that at home! It’s against the law! The Extraordinary Adventures of Cast-Iron Man Yes, that’s it! Remember his catchphrase? “Cast iron is heavy... but brittle!” Why did you bring it up? I don’t know, I just remembered it... The Great Quotes of the Wise Leader Malcolm, your mother is not old enough just yet to forget the title of your favorite book... And that one has never been your favorite, because it doesn’t have any pictures. The Farm and Things That Grow There What farm? You hate digging around in the dirt! You’ve changed a lot over the years, Malcolm - you never lied to me before! Actually, it’s a pity we see each other so rarely these days. When you were little, we used to take the longest walks and you told me about all kinds of things... That was so long ago! One time you caught a glimpse of the sunset through the smog, and said that now it would be your favorite place to go walking! Do you remember where it was? On the embankment Right! Even though we later found out that the sunset was actually the reflection of a warehouse fire, it was beautiful. I remember every moment! At the railway station Are you talking nonsense on purpose? So that I don’t burst into tears at the memory of it? Don’t, Malcolm, I can control myself! You underestimate me! At Pesticide subway station Honey, you’re head is a mess just like it was before! How could you have seen the sunset underground? At the Young Patriot Amusement Park Malcolm, there is no such park in our city, you’re making things up again. Gloria, I think we should stop this! Malcolm, why are you calling me by my name? I’m your mother! Gloria! Look at me! I’m not Malcolm, I’m Evan! I'm sorry I had to lie, but I was worried about your health! Evan? But... Wait a minute! You really are Evan! But where is Malcolm? He was right here! Gloria! Malcolm wasn’t here... What do you mean? He was here, he was here! He was... my son... Come on, try to remember! A car crash... I remember a car crash... Oh no! I remember now... Malcolm died in the crash, and I survived... I’m so sorry, Gloria. It’s all so fuzzy... I felt so much pain... I... Was I in hospital? I remember doctors! Yes, Gloria, you were in hospital. You were treated by the best doctors. You saw things that weren’t there, like you’re seeing now, but you were cured and even came back to work! But I saw Malcolm as clear as I see you now! Oh my God... Has it started again? Gloria, you need to start with your treatment again! So that’s why everyone was looking at me like I was crazy! That’s why I couldn’t contact my son... It feels like I can see things clearly again! Evan, I need to be readmitted into hospital... You’ve made the right decision, Gloria! I’m happy for you. ->$7502 Thank you, Gloria! You shouldn’t have... Oh, I should! Thanks again. I’ll go call Dr. Binet, maybe he has a place for me on his ward... Good luck, Gloria. Get well! ->$6920 I’m sorry Gloria, but I can’t accept it. Don’t make me. You have a big heart, Evan Redgrave! It’s a pity there aren’t more people like you out there! I’ll go call Dr. Binet, maybe he has a place for me on his ward... Good luck to you, Gloria. Get well! Wait... You’re not my son! You’re not Malcolm! Who are you? Finally, you’ve come to your senses! I’m your colleague Evan! But why did you pretend to be my son? Justify yourself I had to! You were having hallucinations! What are you talking about, what hallucinations? Do you think I’m crazy? Well... sort of, yes! WHAT?! I’ve never been so insulted! I’m going to tell Malcolm - he’s the middleweight boxing world champion, I’ll have you know! He’ll beat you to a pulp, you scoundrel! Do not come near me any more! Young man, there is no excuse for this kind of childish talk! You should learn to take responsibility for your actions! ->$9385 Because I’m fed up with your “my son this, my son that”! ->$9388 You heard me! I’ve had it up to here with you and your damn Malcolm! We’ll talk later. Get back to work! There’s nothing to be happy about, Evan. I’m so confused... You helped me and I want to thank you. I have some money that I was saving as a gift for my son. I want you to take it. Gloria, do you remember Rabindranath Wright? He worked here before I came. Of course, he's Hank's brother. But I haven't seen him in a long time. Tell me about him. Well, he was pretty ordinary... Didn't stand out. He was always joking with his brother. Worked fine. Then he stopped coming to work. I assumed he got sick or got transferred. Hank mentioned something about that, but I had my own problems at the time. And then he started drinking and getting rude. ->$10334 Hmm. A little while before he disappeared, Rabindranath got an award for exceeding the appeals recycling quota. He was summoned to the boss’s office. Thanks, Gloria. How are you, Gloria? Better already, Evan! Thank you. I can imagine how unpleasant it was for you to talk to a crazy woman... No, not at all! You're not... Crazy, Evan, crazy... A crazy old woman with dreams of a non-existent son... But that's all in the past! Are you sure? Yes, of course. They’re already waiting for me at the hospital. I’ll get some rest, undergo treatment then come back to work. I'm sure everything will be alright. I hope so! After everything that happened, I’m not sure anymore. I just hope this doesn’t happen again... Anyway, thank you. You're welcome, Gloria. Get well soon! Thanks for helping, Evan! Hello, my name is Evan! Oh hi, Evan! I’m Arthur Mitchell, the janitor. They got me so liquored up I don’t remember how I got home. Some friends you have. They could have helped! Ah, it’s nothing! I probably tripped on the way back... Or stumbled into a police patrol... Or maybe I got into a fight - my jaw is killing me and I’ve got a couple of teeth missing. You probably shouldn’t drink so much. It’ll be okay in time for the wedding! The main thing is that the boss doesn’t notice. I'm going to clean his office tonight. Can you imagine what an honor that is? On the first day, no less! Hi, Arthur! Oh, hi! How did you know my name? It’s me, Evan! We’ve already met! Ah, I see! You must have been in the bar yesterday when I was with my pals. Did you happen to see who tanned my hide so bad? How am I going to clean the boss’s office tonight? And it’s my first day, no less! Oh, hi! You know my name? Yes, your name is Arthur, you’re a janitor, it’s your first day here and this evening you’re going to the boss’s office. My name is Evan. Are you from the personnel department, or what? How do you know all this? Ah, who cares? Hello, Evan! ->$11075 ->$9413 I know you’re also new here, but maybe you know what this Supergame is all about? No idea! Chess or checkers, I guess... Personally, I’m only into drinking games. Did you see what happened today? They went and fed a bunch of people to the Shredder! What kind of Supergame is it, where people end up shredded like cabbage? I’m shocked myself! I’m supposed to clean up the whole mess afterwards! And on my first day to boot! They could have at least given me an assistant! Assistant? I’m telling you that people were turned into mincemeat because of some stupid game! How’s that my business? I don’t play games like that, so I don’t care. If the losers can’t handle the grind, then the grind will handle them. Get it? Is there actually a story behind that or are you single? I had a girlfriend, but turns out she’s a bitch. We just broke up today. How come? You just can’t trust ‘em! ->$12966 Do you like working as a janitor? It’s only my first day! I’m still having a look around. ->$9438 ->$11252 Haven’t seen him yet. Oh, right! I found it in my pocket today! I was wondering where it came from… Here you are! Thank you, Arthur! ->$9746 Hello, I am Evan Redgrave. I was recently transferred to this floor. Hi. Are you talking about the Shredder? “Shredder”?! Get outta here with your talk like that. This machine is the best thing in the friggin’ Ministry! Aren’t you afraid of saying things like that? What do I have to be afraid of? I’ve overhauled this so-called “Shredder” twice with my eyes closed, and I know it like the back of my three-fingered hand! Where could they find another mechanic like me? ->$9460 ->$9462 Talk about the unit Invite to the quiz ->$9422 Can you tell me about the Shred... your sweetie-pie? Ooh! She’s the coolest thing I’ve ever seen! There’s no other like her in the whole world! It took 1,000 engineers to put her together! She takes up three floors! Got a processing speed of ten tons a minute! Self-sharpening blades, and enough power to grind metal and stone! Have you tried? When I was younger, sure... Kept trying to find something too tough for her to chew up. And did you find anything? What I found was my one true love! Nothing can stop my sweetie-pie when she gets going! ->$9413 Otto, have you heard about the inter-department competition? You office rats have nothing better to do, so you keep coming up with nonsense like that! But have you heard of it? I have no idea what the competition is about! To see who can shuffle the most papers around or something. I only know that I have to clean my sweetie-pie after every game! They toss stuff into her and I’m left to clear up the mess! ->$6747 ->$6265 ->$9480 Do you have a family? Family? What for? Take a look at my sweetie-pie! The curves, the grace! Who do you want to know about? ->$10588 What can you tell me about Gloria? That lady who goes on and on about her son? I think she’s a bit off her rocker... Why do you think so? Look at me. Do I look like a boy? Old enough at least to be her husband, even though she's not my type... but a son? She’s clearly not in her right mind! ->$9492 What can you tell me about Hank? I hate that idiot! He’s been coming here every day for the last week to stand beside my sweetie-pie and bawl his eyes out. She could get rusty! Why does he cry? Hell if I know! Ask him yourself! ->$9498 What can you tell me about Rose? The “suckretary”? Now that’s someone I’d gladly feed to my sweetie-pie! ->$6661 Just to see who wins. Rose is as hard as rock! She’ll devour anyone for that Supergame of hers without batting an eye. I’ve seen all sorts of horrors, but that witch causes me to come out in goosebumps... and drop a crowbar, as it were. Is there no way to reign her in? She only listens to the boss - everyone else means nothing to her. We’re just numbers on a scoreboard. Do you know anything about the boss? Listen, I’m a mechanic! As far as I’m concerned, the farther I am from the superiors, the better. The deal is, he doesn’t mess around here, I don’t mess around in his office. End of story! ->$6189 ->$9510 Do you talk to other mechanics in the Ministry? We should all mind our own business, kid. I’m responsible for my sweetie-pie, and she works great! Rabindra nathrite anksbrother... what language are you speaking right now? The brother of the guy who visits your sweetie-pie every day and cries. He had a brother? I never would’ve guessed. Well, and he thinks his brother fell in... Go on! He fell into my sweetie-pie? Well, yes. What can I say? It happens. Stay at home There’s not enough time for a walk. Go to the square Walk around the building Go to the guard's partner Walk the streets Go to the old river port Go to Fat Tony's house Go and find good company for Pete Ferguson's party Go look for a candidate for the quiz Go to Nathan Kehler's shop Go to the factory for plastic wrap Go to the library for the “The Philosophy of Suffering” book Buy a camera from the building manager Go to the dog show Get a certificate for Stephen Dawking Go to 6 Krushvice St. Search for the merchant that Marwitz mentioned There is a raging sea of people on the central square. Somewhere in the distance, a speaker is shouting at the top of his lungs. His speech, amplified by the loudspeakers, carries over the heads of the crowd. “There can be no mercy for the bloody mercenaries of the Boreans, these rotten murderers, spies, saboteurs and pests!” Thousands of voices echo this call. Chant “All hail the Leader!” You shout with the crowd until you lose your voice. Exhausted from the stress and deafened by the cries, you go home. ->$11420 Remain silent and watch Nearby a nondescript man in a gray coat looks carefully over the crowd, and then nods to you. Nod back The stranger glances meaningfully at a backstreet and walks briskly into it. Go after him When you approach, the Gray Coat shows you a policeman's badge and asks if you're his backup. Lie and say you are his backup The policeman informs you that he spotted a particularly dangerous wanted criminal in the crowd. He asks you to help apprehend the criminal, because his partner is on the other side of the square. ->$11299 Having established a joint plan of action, you make your way through the crowd to the tall man and pin him down. The terrorist is led away, and you quickly leave the square before the Gray Coat decides to ask which department you are from. Lie and say you have another mission The man nods knowingly and returns to the square. A few moments later there is a deafening explosion! The gateway shields you from the debris, but what you see on the square will give you nightmares until the end of your days... You tell him honestly that you came to the square by chance and do not have any connection to the police. He nods in understanding. Offer help The Gray Coat thinks for a moment and then lays his cards on the table: he's spotted a particularly dangerous wanted criminal in the crowd. He needs help apprehending him, because his partner is on the other side of the square. Having established a joint plan of action, you make your way through the crowd to the tall man and pin him down. The terrorist is led away, and the Gray Coat writes down your name, salutes you, then gets into the car with the criminal and rides away. Excuse yourself and leave You leave the backstreet and go home. After walking a few blocks you hear an explosion. You shudder to think what might have happened if you had stayed there a few more minutes. Stay put Ignoring the man, you continue to stare around for a few more minutes. When you start to think that you might as well pop in to the nearest bar, there is a deafening explosion near you. The statue of the Leader in the square blows into pieces, one of which hits you in the head. Dying, you wonder, “Have I just been killed by the Leader's mustache?” Go home You get home safe and sound. Disappear into the crowd You have seen this look before. That's how the Special Department hounds look when they spot their prey, and you really don't want to be brought in for questioning for not praising the Leader along with the rest. You blend in to the crowd and leave the square in a roundabout way. You're stopped in your tracks by the sounds of an explosion behind you and screams for help. ->$14811 Through the clouds of smoke you see an old man lying in a pool of blood. Your gaze falls on the expensive watch in his vest pocket. On the pavement next to him sits a girl squeezing his hand. You can make out the words “help my grandpa!” through her sobs. Take the watch The gold watch on a chain leaves the old man's pocket and goes into yours. You leave for home immediately, dropping by a pawnshop on the way. ->$11406 You leave the square and get home safely. Continue watching Ignoring the man, you continue to stare around for a few more minutes. When you start to think that you might as well pop into the nearest bar, there is a deafening explosion near you. The statue of the Leader in the square blows into pieces, one of which hits you in the head. Dying, you wonder, “Have I just been killed by the Leader's mustache?” Continue the walk ->$7842 ->$6920 ->$6933 You manage to heave the old man over your shoulder and carry him to the nearest ambulance. The paramedics bandage him up quickly; he will live. Dirty streets, shady characters in the doorways and the overbearing stench of poverty. The guard's home is located on the very outskirts of the city. The End Signs of repair work indicate that the owner is trying to look after the place. You stop in front of a cracked, but still sturdy, oak door. Knock on the door Scratching the belly under his T-shirt, a huge unshaven man opens the door. Despite his menacing appearance, he looks friendly and gestures you in. He gives you a stony stare. Seeing your feebleness and the fact that you came alone, the man opens the door and nods his head, inviting you inside. He looks you up and down, nods agreeably and asks why you came to his home. Enter without knocking You push the door and it gives way, but a sharp blow from the other side brings it back to its place. The foot you used to try to jam the door open responds with a crunch. A coarse male voice from behind the door proposes that you get out of here you want to stay alive. Apologize for the intrusion You apologize for your tactlessness and say that you are here on a business visit and would like to see the owner. Scratching the belly under his T-shirt, a huge unshaven man opens the door and gives you a stony stare. You feel nervous and can't string two words together. Pretend to leave then come back You try to enter the building again, overcoming the resistance from inside. This time the door swings back into place even faster, and hits you in the face. Blood gushes from your nose onto your shirt, and you can hear a bar lowering behind the door. The door is locked. It's a little damp inside. There are bottles and cigarette butts everywhere, and the smell of poverty hangs in the air. A few short-cropped children's heads look out of the back room, but immediately hide. Tell him why you’re here As soon as you mention the guard who sent you, the man begins to frown and grind his teeth. He says he's not going to help that jerk, and that he can kiss his... Well, apparently he's not in the mood for a heart to heart talk. Ask for more details A little push was all it took to get the man to tell you everything. Before becoming a security guard, he worked as a debt collector for a local gangster. When his boss was killed, he got a job in security and started selling confiscated items to pay off his card debts and buy booze. Everything was going well until his new partner found out about it and threatened to tell everything. So he had to return to his usual line of business - kicking the debts out of poor bastards. And if his former partner sends someone else to him, he’ll regret it. You thank him for the information and leave the building. You don't carry enough confidence in your voice to get the man talk. Leave the building Your task was to find the partner, and you found him. You politely say goodbye and leave the building. Turning from People's Wrath Square into an alley, you notice a group of children beating up a homeless man. A man in a raincoat is standing next to them and writing something down in a notebook. Coming closer, you decide to ask what's going on. The man in the raincoat puts away his notebook and says that this is a group of children from the Young Guard patriotic camp. The children are undergoing a course of basic military training and patriotic education. This is their first trip to the city. The counselor is dissatisfied with their behavior and considers them too passive. They give a bonus for each child brought to them. The camp brings the children up to be model citizens. The counselor silently continues to look at the children. The homeless man has not moved in a long time. You turn around and go home. The End There's nothing interesting near the building. You return home. You can hear children screaming in the background and smell cooking food through the unwashed windows of the first floor apartments. The door to the entrance has been torn off its hinges, and there is a fresh inscription on the wall that reads “The Leader can go fu...” (the end of the phrase has been painted over). A stylish leather sofa is blocking your path on the first floor stairs. A plump, red-faced man is fussing over it. He is trying to drag the sofa upstairs by himself. The neighbor turns to you for help. Help him move the sofa Puffing and panting, you carry the sofa up to the fifth floor. When you get to the final turn, a sharp pain shoots through your back. It's good that your neighbor isn't a complete scumbag, and gives you a bottle of good whiskey for your work. Ask where the sofa came from The neighbor tells you how he accidentally came upon a furniture voucher, and accidentally saw this sofa in a shop, and accidentally had some money with him, but his shifty eyes tell you that he's lying. ->$12501 You tell your neighbor that you don't believe his fairy tale and want to report this chic leather sofa. The neighbor turns pale and starts begging you not to report him. He tells you about his sick wife and her demands to get a “normal sofa”. That he bribed the head of his shop floor, who got the voucher from someone else. The neighbor offers you money for your silence. ->$13343 You give him a meaningful look and nod. The neighbor takes everything he has out of his pocket and continues to push the sofa up the stairs. Refuse the bribe The neighbor breathes a sigh of relief. He says that he only has enough money to pay the utility bills. Then he runs home and comes back with a bottle as a thank you gift. He doesn't ask for any more help with the sofa. You climb the stairs. Report the sofa to the manager The manager listens to you with interest and promises to look into it. He reduces your utility bill as a thank you for taking such a noble stand. He now has a lovely sofa, and you haven’t seen your neighbor since. Go home The neighbor isn't happy about your curiosity and doesn’t hide it. You are unable to persuade him. Don't ask questions and help Deciding that everyone has to do what they can, you help him bring the sofa to the right floor. When you get to the final turn, a sharp pain shoots down your back and you reach the apartment in a half-bent state. It's good that the neighbor isn't a complete scumbag, and gives you a bottle of good whiskey for the trouble. ->$6920 You pass him by. The neighbor mutters incredulously, but leaves you alone. The End You come to the old river port. A few years ago, a fire destroyed most of it. All that remains is a quay, a brick warehouses and a few structures behind the railway tracks. The scientist that Rakovich talked about lives around here. But how can you find him? Shout “Professor, I need insulin!” The light river breeze carries your words into the void. Dogs bark in the distance. Go to the warehouses At first glance, the warehouses seem abandoned. As you come closer, you notice fresh tire tracks on the ground. There is a sign on the fence: “Do not enter”. Go past the fence You take a couple of steps and two large, armed men in uniform appear next to you. You have broken the rules, and the military aren’t happy about it. Your ministerial employee ID saves you from prison, but not from a fine. At least they didn't shoot you. ->$10260 The sign is very clear, so go back. Go to the barge at the pier You see a dirty-faced boy on the shore by the barge. He is kicking pebbles absentmindedly and is clearly waiting for someone. The boy starts to get nervous when he notices you. Talk to the boy You call out to the boy, but he makes a show of not being able to hear you. ->$10147 The boy reluctantly enters into conversation. You find out that he is an orphan and lives with his grandfather, who at the moment is stealing coal from the warehouse nearby. It’s cold in their cellar, and one bag of coal is enough to stoke the furnace for a whole month! The usual story. Everyone survives however they can. You can’t get the boy to talk. Introduce yourself as a police officer Putting on a stern appearance, you say that you work for the police and that the boy is obliged to answer your questions. It turns out the boy has the right reaction: he kicks you in the groin and runs towards the warehouses shouting “we’ve got trouble!” while you try to straighten up. Go back Inspect the barge The barge looks like an old rusty washtub. It looks like some homeless people have settled in here - you find a couch and a bunch of old things in one of the cabins. ->$10158 The only thing of worth is a bunch of wires, which local residents must have wanted to sell for scrap. Take the wires Rightly so - why throw away old wires? Maybe you could put them to use? There’s nothing more to take. Go along the railroad tracks The tracks stretch deep into the port. One branch dives off into a dark tunnel, and the other leads to an abandoned freight shed - a temporary warehouse where carriages were once loaded. Go to the tunnel The entrance to the tunnel is closed off with heavy gates. Someone removed the wires from the control panel - copper is highly-prized at scrap recycling centers. Try to open the doors with your hands You try to open the gate with your hands, but only manage to tear off a couple of your nails. Connect wires The “foolproof” design that only allows each wire to be inserted into a certain slot helps you bring the console back to life. Bulbs light up, and the gate opens and lets you into the shade of the tunnel. Enter the tunnel Go back Go to the freight shed Despite the general desolation, the old building looks in reasonable shape: the windows are intact, the graffiti on the walls are almost invisible, and the door is closed with a robust lock. Try to break the lock Learning to pick locks was a very good idea. You open the door with ease. There is a small office inside: cabinets, desks, books and a huge safe. Look around the room Suddenly you hear voices from the other side of the building. It could be the owners of the safe, or it could be thieves. You retreat to the door and take a book from the table along the way. Raindrops begin to fall from the sky, muffling your steps and letting you escape unnoticed. The scientist's laboratory is definitely not here, so there’s no point going back to the shed. You decide not to risk it and leave. The scientist's laboratory is definitely not here, so there’s no point going back to the shed. You’ve never had to deal with a tumbler plate lock before. You can't open it, so you go back to where you started. Stop searching Searching the port for a person who doesn’t want to be found is a hopeless undertaking. You need to rethink your strategy and come back later. The far side of the tunnel has collapsed, but there’s light in a side utility room. Inside it you find an old man with disheveled gray hair. He obviously wasn't expecting you, but he tries not to show his fear. The old man mutters his name and tries to complain. After a short dialogue it becomes clear that this was who you were looking for - a chemist who carried out his experiments far from the ministries and universities. Ask why he is hiding The old man says that pacifists have to make any and all sacrifices for the their ideals. So he left the Ministry of Science embroiled in scandal after learning that his inventions were being used by the military. He had to go into hiding when his wife reported him for his laboratory in the attic of their home. Ask him for insulin You talk about children who are suffering from diabetes and will die without insulin, and ask the old man to name a price for the leftover drugs. Without hesitation, the old man agrees to give the medicine still stored in the warehouses to a good cause. He says that there are at least a dozen such warehouses, meaning that the children will have enough medicine. For a while, anyway. You can inform Rakovich that you have fulfilled her request and come to an agreement. It won't cost her a penny. The End It is usually easy to find the manager, but today you look around the whole building and even ask the neighbors - he is nowhere to be found. Some of the tenants recall that the manager was going to the Ministry. Every public office comes with its share of paperwork. After a while you meet the manager. There is anxiety in his voice. Ask about cameras You ask the manager about buying cameras. The manager is not very surprised by your question. He tells you the price. Buy a camera You buy a camera. The manager doesn't care why you need it. ->$11415 ->$11417 You decide to think it over and come back later. ->$11420 Remembering Rose Everdeen's assignment, you decide to walk down the street in search of a candidate for the patriotic quiz. There are a lot of people on the sidewalk. You have to choose who to talk to. Police patrol An officer and several patrolmen are escorting a professor to the station. He was giving a lecture and, in violation of the Ministry of Science's directives, presented the argument that there may be more than two digits after the decimal in Pi. You explain your problem to the officer - it seems you caught him in a good mood. He offers the professor the opportunity to atone for his sins by participating in the patriotic quiz. The professor instantly agrees and looks at you in the hope that you will rid him of the company of the police. Agree to take the professor You agree to the officer's proposal. The patrol disappears, and the professor warmly thanks you for setting him free - by law, any black mark against his name could get him fired from the university. As a sign of gratitude, he gives you a book he had on him at the time of his arrest, and promises to come tomorrow at the appointed time. Refuse to take the professor You refuse, citing the professor's unreliability as your reason - if he broke the law once, what's to stop him from lying to the police officers and failing to fulfil his promise? The officer agrees with you and, after some thought, orders one of the patrolmen to find out where to sign up. Their department could use some extra patriotism points. Shoe shiner The dirty man covered in shoe polish doesn't look like an expert in patriotic acts and achievements, but he’ll do any job that pays. After hearing you out, he asks for a fee. ->$8092 You easily part with the money and explain where he has to go to participate in the quiz. Don't pay The cleaner goes back to his work, and you go back to looking around the street. Activist at the crossroads Fully occupied with glorifying the Leader, his actions and his magnificent mind, the activist doesn't notice you right away. You tell him what you need, and the activist almost jumps for joy! It turns out that taking part in the patriotic quiz is his childhood dream - he’s been training for it since a young age. He recently even tried to buy a place on the team, but no one agreed to sell it. Sell participation for You tell him that his dream can come true, but not for free. Joyful, the activist immediately gives you the money and asks how to get to the quiz. He almost faints with happiness. Offer free participation The activist almost cries in happiness when he finds out that he'll soon be taking part in the quiz. His joy is infectious, and you go home with a smile on your face. ->$11406 The street quickly empties as it gets dark. Leader's Street, where Anthony Solerno's home stood, is on the other side of the city, in the green zone. Judging by the fact that only important officials and general-level military men are allowed to live there, the former mafioso clearly had a good thing going on. Use the subway Luckily there are vacant seats in the car, so you can relax and even take a nap. Not enough time. Order a taxi Estimating the distance to Anthony's home, you decide to order a taxi. The taxi driver confidently dives into the city alleys, and for a while you ride in silence. A few blocks later you notice that the car isn't going to Leader's Street, but rather in the opposite direction. Tell the driver that he’s lost his way Turning on all of your charm and politeness, you inform the driver that Leader's Street is on the other side of the city. In response, the taxi driver hits the brakes, turns to you and starts ranting about the kind of idiot who’d try to teach him, Alejandro Perez, how to drive a taxi. He promises to throw you out of the car if you say another word. Insist that you’re right It turns out that Alejandro Perez wasn't joking. He forcefully pulls you out of the car and hits you on the head a couple of times, then leaves you in an unfamiliar place. If you go to Leader's Street with no money and with two black eyes, you’ll certainly be arrested by the police. It looks like your trip is over for today, and you wind your way back home. ->$11345 A few bills and your apologies calm the taxi driver down and he continues his journey. It turns out that he actually did know a shortcut, and he rightfully earned a tip. You don't have enough money. The taxi driver throws you out of the taxi. Ride in silence Relying on the driver's experience, you silently examine the unfamiliar landscapes outside the window. Following a chain of narrow streets, he brings you directly to Anthony's home, cutting a good ten kilometers off the journey. ->$14823 Go home The building where Fat Tony used to live has seen better days. Once a beautiful mansion in the classical style, the empty windows and burnt-out walls now gape at you. There was a fire here not long ago - the police are still standing guard and not letting onlookers into the building. Ask a passerby what happened The neighbors have come to see what happened, dressed in whatever they had on. A stately man in a general's tunic and flowery shorts scans you from head to toe scornfully and turns away, refusing to even talk to you. Ask the police what happened As politely as you can and without making any sudden movements, you ask the nearest policeman about what happened. Luckily enough, he turns out to be a big man with the simple face of a country-dweller. He says that the cause of the fire is unknown, and that a woman and a child were killed in the incident. Ask to examine the crime scene You ask the big guy to let you in, and he’s more than willing to oblige - for a fee. ->$10237 The banknote instantly disappears into the policeman's palm and he lifts up the cordon tape for you. You don't have enough money for a bribe. Fat Tony will have to hear this sad story without any of the details. Go home You’ve found out enough: Anthony Solerno's home burned down, and his family is dead. People these days aren’t generally in the habit of shooting the messenger - though that may not be the case when it comes to former mobsters. Once luxurious, the building is now a miserable sight. Burnt furniture, soot-covered paintings, wet books and ash everywhere. Examine the hall Among the general mess, you notice a half-burned painting and something metal behind it. Pushing aside the work of art, you find a wall safe. Crack the safe There’s no such thing as luck. If you know how to open locks, then it’s sure to come in handy some day. Inside the safe you find the remains of some papers and a slightly burnt bundle of banknotes. They are almost intact, which is lucky. You’ve never had to deal with a tumbler plate lock before. You can't open it. Leave the safe alone It's unlikely that the police missed the safe and left something of value inside. Inspect the bedroom A luxurious four-poster bed takes up almost the entire room, and there isn't a lot of other furniture. Examine the bed You don't find anything interesting Examine the bedside table Examine the dressing table There's nothing here. You find a gold ring in the bottom drawer. ->$10260 Inspect the nursery Leave the building The End You go out for a short walk and find yourself in the red light district. In one of the alleys, a girl with bright makeup and in an unpatriotic mini-skirt blocks your way on a narrow sidewalk. She offers to spend some time with you in her room nearby. ->$7502 You don’t have that much money with you. You exchange a few banknotes for a couple of dozen minutes of pleasure in the girl's room. You never ask her name. When leaving the building, you run into a ostentatiously-dressed man who hands you his business card. Take it The man smiles and tells you to call any time. Feeling pleasant fatigue throughout your whole body, you go home. ->$10274 You ignore him and go outside. The girl you just left shouts from the window that you forgot your wallet on the table. She throws it straight into your hands. Of course, it’s missing a few bills, but in their place is a card with a phone number and the silhouette of a naked woman. Not wanting to return to this den of sin, you go home. Apart from some change, you find a card with a phone number and the silhouette of a naked woman in you wallet. The girl, inspired by the fact that you spent your last money on her, slipped in a fifty and her business card. You never know where luck might find you. Feeling a pleasant fatigue in your whole body, you go home. ->$6920 A pimp appears next to the girl. He really wants to know the reason for your refusal. Not interested in such services The man slips a business card into your jacket pocket and tells you to call whenever you decide to take an interest. This incident and the pimp's nasty smile have spoiled your mood, so you go home. You don't like this girl The pimp slips a business card into your jacket pocket, while telling you that he has a lot of other girls who are eager to get acquainted, and that you can call him any time. This incident has spoiled your mood, so you go home. Don't like girls in general Not at all embarrassed, the pimp takes out his business card and slips it into the breast pocket of your jacket, saying that he doesn’t just have girls, but also young men and inanimate objects for “special purposes”. Not wishing to find out what that means, you leave in a hurry. Say you're impotent Even this killer of an argument doesn’t make the pimp flinch. He literally pushes his business card into your hands and tells you that he has experts who are even capable of arousing the interest of the Leader himself. The End Nathan Kehler is glad to see you and asks about the purpose of your visit. Sell ministerial bonds Nathan will buy the bonds for Sell Nathan buys the bonds. Don't sell Nathan is not happy that you changed your mind. Buy a fake pass Nathan gives you a pass. ->$11415 Buy a memory unit Nathan gives you a memory unit. Buy a wig Nathan gives you a wig. He smiles slyly and straightens his top hat. Buy a bottle of whiskey Nathan gives you a bottle. He advises you to never mix soda with single malt whiskey. Arrange soap deliveries You tell the story of children from the House of Misfits. Nathan shakes his head sympathetically. He is ready to help the poor dirty children for ->$7502 Nathan gives his word (he can be trusted) that he will deliver boxes of soap to the House of Misfits. ->$6189 Go home TRADE Talk about hiring mercenaries to shut down the plant You tell Nathan the story of the Suburb and ask about people who would be prepared to protect the interests of the workers. ->$8092 Nathan says: “What lovely numbers! They reminded me of another number: 14-061-789. Call it, and they’ll definitely hear you out.” Nathan sympathizes, but he is not ready to sacrifice his own wellbeing for the sake of others. He knows some people who are, but this information costs money. Go home Using the address that DeSalvo gave you, you find a factory that has definitely seen better times. There is dirt, desolation and ruin everywhere. There is a warehouse to your right, a production building to your left, and a neat factory management building a little further away. Go to the factory management You search the offices and find the director of the factory inside one of them. After you mention DeSalvo, he straightens up, blinks and falls into a stupor. ->$13831 The director fixes his gaze on your and says that the factory has been having minor difficulties for a long time - the equipment has been taken away, and the workers and supplies are long gone. The only way he can help is to give you a voucher to get the wrap from the warehouse. There may be some reserves left. As he continues scrutinize you, the director gradually turns pale. Sweat appears on his face, and he sinks to the floor. You have to raise the alarm and call an ambulance for him, but you definitely won't be getting any wrap today. ->$10353 As he comes out of his stupor, the director quickly sweeps the money into a drawer and fills in the voucher to get the goods from the warehouse. The director's eyes light up with greedy fire, but the money you have won't be enough. Yell at him You shout at the director, describing his heritage and sexual proclivities in great detail. Having gone through several color changes during your speech, the director reaches out a trembling hand with a voucher to get the wrap from the warehouse. Go to the warehouse Scratching his stomach, the warehouse manager appears from the far corner. He says that, firstly, he won't give you anything without a voucher for the goods, and, secondly, there’s nothing to give anyway. Go to the production building The door is locked. Leave the factory and go home The warehouse manager is napping on some boxes when you enter. Opening one eye, he first asks if you’ve brought a voucher for the goods. Ask why he needs a voucher if there is nothing to give He says that without a voucher, he cannot officially refuse to give you the goods. That's how it works. Show him the voucher The warehouse manager impales the voucher on a sharp pin, writes its number in his journal and then throws his hands up again: “We’re outta wrap”. Threaten to complain to the director The man laughs so hard that he has to spend a considerable time wiping away the tears with a dirty handkerchief. When he gets his breath back, he tells you that the real director ran away long ago, and that the worker sitting in the factory management building is only there so that no one suspects anything when inspections are carried out. Ask where to get the wrap The manager rolls his eyes to the ceiling and tells you that he has two rolls, which he bought for his greenhouse. He could sell a spare roll to a worthy buyer. ->$11083 Leave the warehouse The manager closes the entrance gate tight so that no one disturbs you. He tells you to wait, before disappearing into the darkness of the warehouse. A few minutes later, he brings you a great roll of wrap and lets you out through a secret door that leads from the warehouse directly to the street. Now it is clear why there is nothing left in the warehouse, but you don’t need anything else anyway. Giving you a look full of contempt, the warehouse manager spits on the floor, silently turns around and leaves. It seems that your proposal was not to his liking, and the meeting has come to an end. Maybe he will be more accommodating if you come back with a different amount? Go home The city library welcomes you with open space and emptiness. Behind the desk sits a pretty librarian in a gray suit which, despite its best efforts, can’t downplay her attractive figure. She yawns. Ask the librarian about the old book The librarian hears you out, takes a deep breath, gives you a piercing look and starts flipping through some documents. After a couple of minutes, she tells you that “The Philosophy of Suffering” is in a closed-off archive. You would need special permission to get it. Find the book in the catalog yourself You find the card for “The Philosophy of Suffering” in the catalogue, but there is a different book on the shelf: “Mince. 100 Ways to Cook Without Meat”. The book you need is nowhere to be found. Ask for permission Noticing that you are only interested in the book, the girl says: “The department that issued permits closed down 3 years ago. I can't help you. Please move away from the desk, I have a lot of work to do.” Maybe she’s just having a bad day, but you can tell that you won’t get anything else from her today. ->$10375 Using all of your charm, you ask the girl to show a little understanding and help you with the book. She blushes and whispers that permission is just a formality - but there was a flood recently in the secret archive, and all the books have been moved and dumped in another room. Now you’d be lucky not to break your leg in there. Suggest that she searches while you wait here The girl snorts, saying that she has a lot of work as it is and that other customers are waiting for her. You are the only person in the library, but her indignant look tells you that you won't get any help from her today. Offer to help her find the book The girl is delighted and leads you into the depths of the library. There really is a small room with a sofa, with books piled up almost to the ceiling. You find the book quickly, but the sparks that have been flying between you two from the start keep you there a long time. When you return to the reading room, the flushed librarian straightens her hair and, giggling, adds something else to your order: “The World of Antennas. Size Doesn't Matter”. You don’t remember the name of the girl, but you’ve fulfilled DeSalvo's request and can safely go home. Alas, there is not enough time to search. ->$10353 Several crisp bills make the girl forget about permission. After a short time, she brings you the right book. She obviously likes you. You exchange some frivolous hints with her and leave this temple of literature, never to return again. Who goes to the library these days anyway? In a voice ringing with resentment, she tells you that librarians work for love. The love of books, more precisely. They work for an idea, not for money! ->$10147 You convince the girl that you have the right to take the book. Hesitating a bit, she agrees and brings you the book. She didn’t like your official tone, but you don’t really care. The girl sticks her nose up in the air and says that even if the Great Leader himself came here without permission, she won't allow such a violation of order! The country is celebrating Puppy Month, introduced in honor of the Wise Leader's beloved dog. It turns out that a Ministry order grants all employees who visit the exhibition a reward. A large crowd has gathered in the Municipal Palace of Events. There are a lot more people than dogs. You need a special permit for a dog, and it's extremely hard to get one. You notice the familiar profile of Albert DeSalvo in the crowd. He is surrounded by huge, square-shaped men with shaved heads. He is holding a miniature dog the size of a handbag. The procession passes through the hall and disappears into a room with the sign “VIP Males”. The entrance is guarded by a man with an expressionless face. Talk to the guard You approach the guard and try to politely start a conversation. The guard hardly notices you and shows no interest. Bribe the guard Realizing that the guard won't tell you anything interesting, you wander around the exhibition for another half an hour, them go home frustrated by this useless waste of time. Yell at the guard With a barely noticeable motion, the guard hits you in the stomach, carefully moves you aside and returns to his place, leaving you to catch your breath. Rush past the guard Imitating the important and haughty look of high-ranking officials, you try to pass by the guard, but he silently blocks the way. You decide to change tactics and take out your wallet. All guards take bribes, and this one is no exception. He asks what you need. Ask for permission to enter The guard shakes his head in frustration - not even a bribe is enough to get him to allow visitors not on the secret list approved personally by the Wise Leader into “VIP Males”. Ask about DeSalvo The guard says that Comrade DeSalvo is an honorary member of the Helmer Canine Club. They also say that this is his favorite dog and he doesn't let anyone near it. Several people have already lost their lives trying to pet it. ->$13911 Comrade DeSalvo's dog is insured for a six-figure sum, so it is guarded like the Mayor of Helmer. Or better. ->$13914 This dog was gifted to Comrade DeSalvo by the Leader himself as a sign of favor and trust. The dog's name is Spock, after the protagonist of DeSalvo's favorite TV show. ->$11406 I'm in a real hurry, could you please let me cut in? If I let you in ahead of me, I won't live to see next spring! ->$6661 Can't you see? I’m thirty kilos underweight! Here, look at my papers! If they don't give me some vouchers for a high-calorie diet soon, they'll be receiving a complaint from a very unsatisfied pile of bones... Help him Show me your papers. Maybe I can help. Yes, of course. Here, look... Move him aside Well, while you figure out whether you’re going to stand or lie there, I’m going through. Go and steal something! ->$5706 I said I'm not giving you any money, so go and steal something, beggar! Threaten him Have you ever though that someone might make you answer for words like that? Answer to you, scumbag? Go and steal something, I said! ->$10353 I already have! Do you want me to share? Now you're talking... Go and steal something, I say! And why should I let you through? Because people should help each other! Just what kind of people, I wonder? And what do you mean “help”? Explain We’re all citizens of the same country, we live under the same sky, breathe the same air… And that's why you think I owe you something? No, mister. I’m not agreeing to that... Talk nonsense Don't you know of the law of the singularity of twisted internal properties? Uh... Sorry, what? Well, you should look it up, then! You think about that for now, and just I'll go on ahead, okay? No need to be rude, young man… Why do you say I'm rude? Your eyes tell me that you want to cut in line, even though there’s a fragile and defenseless girl right in front of you! Ah, what happened to all the good men?! Mock her Fragile and defenseless? You? Don’t make me laugh! What? How dare you talk to me like that, you freak! ->$10375 I’m sorry. It’s just... When I stand behind you, I’m so overcome by what I see in front of me that I completely lose my head... Unfortunately, I still need it for a meeting with my boss… You’re a naughty one, trying to flatter me like that! You’re lucky I have some free time... I couldn’t let you get into trouble because of me, could I? Young man! The fact that you’re in a hurry is no reason to break with established order. But I'm really in a hurry! And that just emphasizes your lack of discipline! Looks like your supervisor from the Ministry of Labor isn't worth their salt! We didn't tolerate that sort of thing back in my day! ->$11339 Well, you must have been worth your salt if you managed to get a fat ass like that! What?! How dare you, boy! I'm an honored worker of the Ministry! I have 50 years of experience! I remember the Leader when he was still young! Lie convincingly You’re wrong! My supervisor is a wonderful person! He's just waiting for me to bring him some papers so that he can file a report. That's why I'm in a hurry! I can't let him down! Why didn't you just say so? Go ahead! I'm also in a hurry! I have urgent business, too! But you don't see me trying to cut in line! Maybe we should toss a coin? Fine, why not? What do you choose? Emblem Emblem! Aaaand.. You lose! Sorry, friend, rules are rules. Leader Leader! Aaaand... Damn! Fine, go ahead. ->$10984 Evacuation! An bomb went off in the left wing, sector 4.7. Go home, everyone! Attention all employees of the Appeals Distribution Department! Pete Ferguson speaking! Someone has stole securities from my safe! If the thief doesn't come clean by the end of the day, you’ll all be fined! And I’ll keep fining you until I find the shit-eating bastard who dared to rob the Ministry! Hello! Did you know that true beauty lies within us, and not on the surface? Is this another sect? Why, of course not! I represent an international company that can offer you huge profits with minimum effort! What’s it all about? It's simple! You get to lie in bed while your kidney does all the work! My kidney? Sure! Kidney, retina, or other organs. You know, most people have no brain and it doesn't bother them! So you buy organs? Yes, and if you bring a friend you’ll get 20% of the cost of their organs! Is this even legal? ->$8047 “Of course” what? Of course not! Is it safe? Absolutely! But we don't sign any paperwork and don't give any guarantees. Is the money good? There's something of value in every person! ->$6265 You know, I quite like having a full set of organs! Suit yourself! Take my business card, just in case. Yesterday I was in Ferguson's office... Again…? Again, yes. Anyway, I give him my bribes for the day, he opens his safe, and there's a huuuge pile of Patriotic Government Bonds in there! So what? I have a bond too - we were all forced to buy them! Only we’ll never see any money from these “bonds”. It’s all just a fairy tale for suckers. Small fries like us, who only have one bond, won't get a penny. But if you have a whole pile of them and know the right people… Evan Redgrave? Yes, and you are? Your friends sent us. They want you to help us deal a crushing blow to the bureaucracy. Is this some kind of joke? What friends? What blow? What are you talking about? We've been watching you for a long time. Have you heard of The New Tomorrow? Ask about The New Tomorrow The New Tomorrow? Those people who fight the Great Leader? Oh yes, they want to paint us as murderers, terrorists and Leader-haters. But we’re fighting against oligarchy, bureaucracy and human rights violations! Do we even have any oligarchs? See, we’re doing our job! Ask about “the blow” What “blow to the bureaucracy” are you talking about? We're going to blow up the new wing of the Ministry building! When it opens, thousands of new bureaucrats will start working there, and we won't be able to get in the way of their work without harming people! We’re not animals, Evan, and we don't kill people for nothing. So we need to blow it up now, while it’s still empty! Ask what they need We need you to meet our man in the Ministry to give him the microchip and password that activate the bomb. Why me? Like I already said, we've been watching you. You have a strong personality, Evan, and you’re capable of taking real action. There’s so much you can change in the Ministry, Evan! Really change, you know? We need people like you! Why should I do this? Do you like the uncontrolled growth of the bureaucratic apparatus and the simultaneous strengthening of its totalitarian functions? Honestly, your plans aren’t so great either... But we can pay you well! Agree to the offer Wow! That's a lot! OK, I agree! The New Tomorrow gives money today and spares no expense for tomorrow! I'm glad that we came to an agreement. Here's the microchip. The password is “The sky is always blue over the statue of the Leader”. The man you need is Nicholas Page. He's a courier. Got it? Got it. Don't fail us, Evan! The New Tomorrow depends on you! Refuse the offer I'm not going to blow anything up, no matter how much you pay me. Hard times require decisive action! I don't care! Now get out of my home or I’ll call the police! Don’t say that, Evan! We could be of some help to you! Get out! All hail the Leader! ->$8183 Refuse to talk Get out of my home or I’ll call the police! ->$12743 Okay, tell me more… Close the door They say that someone blew themselves up... And that there are casualties. Thank God! We can go home! How can you say that? If you saw my work schedule for today, you’d say the same! Comrade Redgrave. Comrade Ferguson wants to see you. ->$12299 Hello, I'm new here. My name is Evan Redgrave… Damn, my head feels like it's about to explode... What do you want from me, Evan Prickgrave? Well... I just wanted to say hello! Nothing is ever so simple in this world, you know that? What’s your name? ->$6628 Oh!... You're that... What was your name... Prickgrave! Well, yes… Give him money Here you go! No need to do me any favors! That's it, your audience is over! ->$10526 No. I don't lend money to strangers. I already told you that I'm Hank Wright, we work together. What's wrong with you? So what! Well, then get out of here, jerk! And don't come near me again! Say that you don't have money Sorry, friend, they haven't paid me yet! What, not a single coin? Will a fiver do? No need to do me any favors! I have to go! Don't give him money No, sorry! Sure, Evan. ->$11264 How do you mean? I’m John Smith, everyone here knows me! I mean, Tony... I found out where your family is living... Or was living… Mamma mia! Have they moved again? Did you see my Rosabella and Victor? I'm sorry, Fat Tony, but... Fat Tony, per favore! Start with the good. Your old friends remember you. Merda! What's the bad news then? Nobody comes here without me knowing about it. I’m Rose Everdeen, Comrade DeSalvo’s secretary. Comrade Everdeen to you. Evan Redgrave Carl 512 John Smith Carl 452 Gloria Melfi Carl 193 Hank Wright ->$9462 There are a lot more police cars in Helmer than ambulances, so they arrived for Redgrave almost immediately. The officers didn't deny themselves the pleasure of turning out his pockets, hitting his head against door of the car and throwing him inside like a bag of flour. Out of respect for his father's achievements, they didn't beat Evan with their batons in public. They saved that for when they were behind closed doors, back at the precinct. Redgrave, resigned to his fate, watched the scene with indifference, as if from far away. The fate of Evan Redgrave is unknown. His name has been lost among millions of similar denunciations, disappeared under thousands of multicolored seals, evaporated under hundreds of other people's signatures, vanished... His wife and daughter never learned whether he was shot, or died in the cold of the correctional labor camps along with thousands of other unfortunate souls. The death of Redgrave Junior attracted far less attention than that of his father. And who was he, really? Some even said that Caleb had no son, and that this petty upstart was an impostor who just wanted to get his hands on a ministerial apartment. The case was closed so quickly and the cremation carried out so hastily that it seemed like they wanted to get rid of him as soon as possible, like an annoying fly. Less than a month later, nobody even remembered that there had ever been a man called Evan Redgrave. Only his wife and daughter, who remained in distant Redding, remembered him. But they never found out what had led to such a sudden and sad end. A series of terrible explosions shook the city. The relay towers became colossal columns of flame, falling on residents' houses and destroying the streets below. Violent fires raged for several days, and when they were finally put out, the heaps of twisted iron and sad memories were all that remained of Project Heimdall. Over time, the huge relay towers became a familiar part of the urban landscape. Slowly turning rusty and useless, they hung gloomily over the heads of everyone below. And though some said that the towers were only slumbering, still waiting for their master, no one ever found out what they were really built for. A low, pulsating hum droned through the city, filling the air, boring into every mind and bending every single person to its mechanical will - men and women, adults and children, rich and poor. In just a few minutes, the entire population submitted to Heimdall's power. From then on, people thought, felt, wished and did only what was required of them. Little changed in the country. Ministers continued to fight over power, old players were deposed by new ones - each more vain than the last. A lack of principles became a synonym for success. Ordinary citizens remained a bargaining chip in political games, small cogs in the soulless machine of total surveillance and control. The desire for an abstract “common good”, embedded into the minds of citizens, had unexpected consequences. As it turns out, everyone has their own idea of “good”. And this meant that people strived for different, often opposite things. A wave of unmotivated violence swept through the city. People attacked one another at random, sincerely believing that they were doing it “for the common good”. When the wave of violence subsided, leaving behind it blood-soaked streets, the few survivors - shaken to the depths of their souls by the incident - started building a better world. And they did it with such fervor that they forgot about their own basic needs. After only a month, Helmer was a ghost town, where even the most desperate did not set foot. Is a cult of personality really such a bad thing? After all, if someone receives praise to the high heavens, doesn't that mean that they deserve it? The mindless worship of the Wise Leader was replaced by the blind adoration of Evan Redgrave. And if citizens had previously been able to afford to express their disapproval of the current state of affairs, even in a whisper, now no one would have dared to do so. Because having one's “own” thoughts is an unaffordable luxury in a country covered with relay towers. But this is not the end - it is only the beginning! There are still so many places in the world where Heimdall's towers are yet to rise into the skies, and so many countries yet to learn about Evan Redgrave. You can’t force a person to be happy against their will. But what if you forcefully give them free will? What if you instill in them a single thought: “From now on, you make your own decisions and take responsibility for your own actions”? Could it bring about peace? The inhabitants of Helmer were the first to become truly independent - even though their lives weren’t perfect, their choices were often far from ideal, and the consequences of these choices were painful. But they had their own choices, their own happiness and their own “mercy”. The ministers, all of them, were executed, the monstrous and useless bureaucratic apparatus was gradually dismantled, and the Ministry Building was turned into a museum dedicated to senseless cruelty. Luxury. Senseless and merciless. Marble statues, palaces and golden toilets for officials, Persian carpets, mountains of jewels and money, foul pools filled with champagne and caviar, monstrous orgies. The atmosphere of greed and a rotten system drowning in corruption. People stopped seeing anything wrong in it. The worship of one idol over another caused no kind of conflict - the signal from the relays took care of that. The Wise Leader was replaced by the Luxurious Leader - and no one noticed. It's scary when a real psychopath comes to power. And it is a nightmare if his power is limitless. But there are no words to describe the horror when this maniac gains control of your mind. In the hands of DeSalvo, Heimdall towers spread across the country. But they didn’t just force people to obey - from time to time, they transmitted a single emotion at full capacity - pure horror. Beside themselves with fear, people would increasingly hole up in the deepest crevices they could find, like mice. They had less and less time for anything useful. An atmosphere of impending doom, bordering on panic, reigned in the country for a long time. The relays quickly spread across the country. And citizens were processed into biomaterial to create clones. Copies of the “ideal employee” once known as Carl Stein replaced ordinary people - first in Helmer, then throughout the country, and then, step by step, all over the world. And next, the conquest of the solar system and the stars. A perfect, effectively-functioning world has no room for strife or war, hunger or injustice, love or compassion. Hemnitz deceived Evan unashamedly. With the relays at his disposal, he spread them quickly throughout the country and immediately carried out his plan to destroy the population. People dutifully lined up at the Euthanasia Centers. And when there was no one left, the Boreans came and took everything they needed. The once great country became a desert, with the frightening, empty streets of a ghost town. The ruling elite had no use for Evan. After the protective dome disappeared, he was held at gunpoint and thrown out the same window his father had fallen from. “Like father, like son,” they shouted at him in a mocking farewell. And only during that fall, in a state of absolute terror, did Evan truly feel free for the first time in his life. Even if only for a fraction of a second. Evan’s sacrifice wasn't in vain. None of the ministers who so passionately wanted unlimited control over the minds of the citizens were able to have it. Who knows how much worse things could have been if Heimdall had fallen into the wrong hands? But in the final seconds of his life, Evan still thought: “Could things have turned out differently? Was there ever any way for me to change something for the better?” Anyone who understood what was happening and who still had free will wisely fled the city, while Evan stayed on the top floor, witnessing the consequences of his choice day after day. Sometimes, in moments of weakness, he thought that punishing himself with the monstrous sight of the massacre below was enough to atone for his sins. But soon, there was no one left to whom he could repent. And so the thought of the Shredder, several floors below, seemed more and more attractive to Evan every day. But what mattered was that the thought was his own. Replacing the Wise Leader - is that not the pinnacle of a Ministry career? The name Redgrave was respected before, but now it inspires awe. Isn't that what you wanted for your son, father? Isn't that the greatness you dreamed of when you created Heimdall? Evan sits at the top of the world and everything belongs to him: from the mountains and rivers to the people and their thoughts. He alone decides what his subjects can feel and want. Evan Redgrave is a god now! And what does it matter that the fate of all gods is loneliness? Evan became a full-fledged member of the new society. When asked about what happened in the last days of the Great State, he would only smile and stay silent. He had no wish to recall his time spent in the Ministry, or the nightmares that he witnessed and took part in. Evan lost himself in real work and finally reunited with his family. Until the very end of his life, he tried to erase from his memory the faces of his superiors and colleagues, wishing fervently that he could stop hearing military marches in the distance and the roar of a thousand voices chanting: “All Hail the Wise Leader!” Evan no longer denied himself a thing. Luxury, wine, parties and girls filled the void in his heart. And although he always felt second-rate next to his boss, being God’s number two wasn't so bad. And all you need to do is abandon your own ambitions, bury the memory of your father and learn to shut your eyes to the problems of ordinary people. So let the champagne flow like a river, and may the music never stop! Turn it up louder! Let it silence the contempt that you feel for yourself at the bottom of your heart.
